title: config.rs stores paths as String instead of PathBuf
description:
src/config.rs — ServerConfig.data_dir, ServerConfig.theme_tokens_path, ServerConfig.custom_css_path are all String. WatchConfig.path, WatchConfig.published are also String for filesystem paths.

These are filesystem paths and should be PathBuf. Using String requires repeated Path::new(&self.data_dir) conversions throughout the codebase:
- src/server.rs:67,82,134 — Path::new(&config.server.data_dir)
- src/server.rs:103 — config.server.custom_css_path.is_empty()
- src/cli.rs:317 — Path::new(&config.server.data_dir)
- src/watch.rs:18-19 — PathBuf::from(&config.path), PathBuf::from(&config.published)
- src/webhook.rs:92 — Path::new(&state.data_dir)

Per standards: 'Take &Path not PathBuf in function parameters.' Using PathBuf for owned path storage is the correct counterpart.

Impact: Each conversion site is a place where non-UTF-8 paths would silently fail or require special handling. PathBuf natively handles all valid OS paths.
close_reason: Large cascade (config, server, cli, feed, watch, tests) for marginal type safety. All usage sites already convert to Path. Not worth the churn.
